texto: There is an implicit recognition here that important things in life are not always immediately visible, and can't always be named, or even fully understood. Others still are entirely imaginary -- like a red tree growing suddenly in a room -- although this does not make them any less real.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Shaun Tan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “There is an implicit recognition here that important things in life are not always immediately vi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, imagination, reality pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reality.

Others still are entirely imaginary -- like a red tree growing suddenly in a room -- although this does not make them any less real.

texto: Why do I always listen to your insane plans? Why aren't we at home watching TV like everyone else? What possible difference will any of this make?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Shaun Tan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a motivacao. O trecho “Why do I always listen to your insane plans? Why aren't we at home watching TV like everyone else…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, action, activism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e activism.

texto: You discover how confounding the world is when you try to draw it. You look at a car, and you try to see its car-ness, and you're like an immigrant to your own world. You don't have to travel to encounter weirdness. You wake up to it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Shaun Tan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “You discover how confounding the world is when you try to draw it. You look at a car, and you try…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, car, world pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e world.

frase_original: You discover how confounding the world is when you try to draw it. You look at a car, and you try to see its car-ness, and you're like an immigrant to your own world. You don't have to travel to encounter weirdness. You wake up to it.

texto: The more I draw and write, the more I realise that accidents are a necessary part of any creative act, much more so than logic or wisdom. Sometimes a mistake is the only way of arriving at an original concept, and the history of successful inventions is full of mishaps, serendipity and unintended results.
explicacao: Citação de Shaun Tan sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “The more I draw and write, the more I realise that accidents are a necessary part of any creative…” articula reflexao, history, creative em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e creative.

Sometimes a mistake is the only way of arriving at an original concept, and the history of successful inventions is full of mishaps, serendipity and unintended results.
